ogTitle: Bugle Ranges vs Bumbunga: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bugle Ranges and Bumbunga.
heading: Bugle Ranges vs Bumbunga — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bugle Ranges, South Australia and Bumbunga, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bugle Ranges has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Bumbunga (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bugle Ranges or Bumbunga safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bugle Ranges has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Bumbunga scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bugle Ranges scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Bumbunga scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bugle Ranges or Bumbunga?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bugle Ranges has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
